discovery.title: Find printers
discovery.networkHint: First three octets of a /24 (e.g. 192.168.1) — scans only those hosts, faster. Multiple: new lines or commas. Leave empty to scan from your network interfaces.
discovery.networkInvalid: Invalid format: use three octets (e.g. 192.168.1) or four ending in .0 (192.168.1.0).
discovery.emptyHint: Tap Search to discover printers on the network.

phase.fallback_prepare: No hits — trying common home subnets…

discovery.webNoScan: LAN scan only works in the installed desktop app, not in the browser.
discovery.newPrintersPreview: New printers ({{count}}) — tap the green plus to save; unconfirmed ones are removed when you tap Done

printer.deleteConfirmDescription: {{name}} will be removed from the farm list. The printer itself is not changed.
printer.removeOnDoneHint: Marked for removal — tap Done to apply, or tap the green plus to keep
printer.directUrlInvalid: Enter a host or full URL with port.
printer.alreadyInFarm: This address is already in the farm.
printer.offlineAuthHint: Moonraker returned 401/403. Enter the API key if one is configured, or add this PC’s IP to trusted_clients in moonraker.conf.
printer.offlineUnreachableHint: No response (timeout, firewall, wrong URL/port, or the host is not on this network).
printer.upstreamNewerMoonraker: A newer Moonraker tag is on GitHub: {{tag}}
printer.upstreamNewerKlipper: A newer Klipper tag is on GitHub: {{tag}}

statusHint.dirtyBuild: Uncommitted changes in the reported firmware version (dirty build).
statusHint.offlineAuth: Moonraker rejected the request (401/403). Check API key and trusted_clients.
statusHint.offlineUnreachable: No reply from the host (timeout, firewall, or wrong address).
statusHint.klippyNotConnected: Klipper is not connected to Moonraker (service stopped, crashed, or not started).
statusHint.klippyBusy: Klipper not ready for a job yet (state: {{state}}).
statusHint.errorUnknown: Printer is in an error state; check Moonraker and Klipper logs.

settings.title: Settings
settings.farmNameHint: Shown in the top bar. Saved when you close this dialog.
settings.developerModeHint: Shows diagnostics export, raw logs in the printer hub, and full error details.
settings.devToolsHint: Copy JSON to the clipboard. Printer list omits API keys; the full bundle includes persisted my3dfarm* storage — review before sharing.
settings.applyHostHintUsername: Auto-fill username from host hint (Moonraker system_info)
settings.hostHintsBlurb: Built-in hints match Raspberry Pi / Orange Pi / BTT-style boards. Extend via user overlay JSON (see docs).
settings.massKeyHint: Uses the same one-time password for every printer (typical for a uniform fleet). Per-printer user/port overrides still apply.
settings.baselineHint: Capture and compare resolved Klipper config from the printer hub. Full diff/apply comes later.

hub.sshAuthFailedHint: The host answers on the SSH port, but key login failed. Set the login (and optional overrides), then use the one-time password to install your public key.
hub.sshProbeUnreachable: SSH: port closed or host unreachable
hub.sshIntro: Install your app public key on the host, then run remote diagnostics and apt previews.
hub.deployKeyThisPrinter: Install public key on this host
hub.sshDesktopOnly: SSH actions require the desktop app (Tauri).
hub.updatesIntro: Managed components from Moonraker; system packages via apt preview over SSH.
hub.detailsPlaceholder: Extended printer information will appear here later.
hub.updatesIntroShort: Host packages, Moonraker-managed repos, and MCU firmware notes.
hub.hostPackagesHintMoonraker: Count from Moonraker’s system updater (same source as Mainsail/Fluidd).
hub.hostPackagesHintSsh: Count from apt on the host over SSH (after apt-get update).
hub.hostSnapshotWeb: Kernel/arch and apt count over SSH are only available in the desktop app with working key login.
hub.hostSnapshotSshOnly: Connect SSH (SSH tab) to load kernel, machine arch, and apt counts from the host.
hub.hostPackagesUnavailable: Could not determine pending package count.
hub.updateManagerBusy: Moonraker update manager is busy — wait for it to finish before upgrading.
hub.upgradeOk: Upgrade requested for “{{name}}”. Refresh if versions do not change immediately.
hub.upgradeAllOk: Requested upgrades for all listed components that had updates.
hub.updaterInvalid: Updater invalid or corrupt in Moonraker — fix on the host before updating.
hub.mcuFirmwareWarningShort: Flashing MCU firmware can brick boards. Follow vendor guides; wrong images break CAN/USB chains.
hub.mcuFirmwareExpertBody: Do not flash from this app. Use KIAUH or your distro workflow for USB-connected MCUs. For CAN toolheads and CAN-bridge mainboards, follow Katapult + Klipper build steps from the linked guides. Double-check menuconfig, UUIDs, and CAN speed before flashing.
hub.devModeForTechnicalDetails: Turn on Developer mode in Settings to see the full message.
hub.operationFailedShort: Something went wrong. Enable Developer mode in Settings for technical details.
hub.hostSnapshotFailed: Could not load host details over SSH.
hub.configNotExtracted: Could not find a resolved config block in the log yet — try a larger tail or restart Klipper.
hub.healthIntro: Network counters from SSH diagnostics; compare consecutive samples for retransmit/drop trends.
hub.healthNeedSamples: Run Diagnostics twice to compare samples.
hub.healthTrendOk: No worsening packet counters detected between last samples.
hub.healthTrendDegrading: Counters increased — check cabling, CAN termination, or host load.
hub.baselineIntro: Save a golden resolved config and compare later.
hub.baselineCaptureFromPrinter: Save extracted config as baseline text

fleet.syncInventoryHint: Fetch update status and host info from each printer (Moonraker API)
fleet.selectModeHint: Tap cards to select. Bulk actions only run on printers that are idle and online.
fleet.queuedSelected: Queued {{count}} printer(s) for update when idle.
fleet.pendingTooltip: Managed software updates: {{software}}. Host packages pending: {{host}}.

errors.uiErrorBody: Try returning to the farm and opening the printer again. If it keeps happening, contact support.
errors.uiErrorDevHint: Enable Developer mode in Settings to show technical details here.
